channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India leads discussions as Brics struggles to form consensus on West Asia conflict
item.description: The formation of a unified stance within Brics on the ongoing Iran-US conflict has encountered significant challenges, as some member nations are directly or indirectly involved in West Asia. Iranian ...

item.title: Document reveals first known photograph of Andrew, Mandelson and Epstein together
item.description: The US Department of Justice has released a photograph showing Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Lord Peter Mandelson with convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein. The image, reportedly taken in Martha's Vi...

item.title: Early Data Shows Teen Social Media Ban Has Had Limited Impact
item.description: Three months after Australia introduced the world-first ban on social media for under-16s, initial data suggests the measure has only slightly reduced use among young Australians. According to parenta...

item.title: UN Warns: Gender Equality Still Elusive Worldwide, Legal Barriers Persist for Women
item.description: Di United Nations don yan saye, even after plenty years of advocacy and law reform, no country for di world don reach full equality between men and women. Dis warning na for International Women's Day ...

item.title: Medieval Danish Cemeteries Show the Sick Were Buried Alongside the Healthy
item.description: A new study examining medieval Danish cemeteries challenges the long-held belief that people with stigmatized diseases were socially excluded. Researchers analyzed 939 adult skeletons from five cemete...

item.title: Middle East Conflict May Lead to Rising Medical Costs in the Philippines
item.description: An economist has warned that the ongoing tensions in the Middle East may lead to a rise in medical costs in the Philippines. Professor Emmanuel Leyco explained that many medical supplies, including sy...

item.title: Ministry of Energy and PURA Meet to Boost Declining Oil and Gas Exploration in Tanzania
item.description: In Dar es Salaam on March 12, 2026, the Permanent Secretary in the Ministry of Energy for petroleum and natural gas, Dr James Mataragio, held an important meeting with the management of the Petroleum ...

item.title: Matt Canavan's Leadership and the National Party's Approach to One Nation
item.description: Matt Canavan has stepped up as the leader of the National Party, prompting questions about whether he has the strategy and political acumen needed to counter the influence of One Nation. Political com...

item.title: ASEAN ministers assure open trade to stabilize regional economy during Middle East conflict
item.description: ASEAN Economic Ministers (AEM) met in Taguig City and reaffirmed their commitment to keeping the region’s markets open for trade and investment amid ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. T...

item.title: Jury Begins Deliberations in Bellwether Trial Against Meta and YouTube Over Social Media Addiction Claims
item.description: In a groundbreaking trial in Los Angeles County Superior Court, a 12-person jury is set to begin deliberations on whether Meta (owner of Instagram and Facebook) and Google-owned YouTube should be held...

item.title: Winter Storm Prompts Snow Alerts in Kitchener, Waterloo, and Cambridge
item.description: Local authorities in the Kitchener-Waterloo-Cambridge region have declared snow events as winter weather conditions intensify. The declaration is intended to alert residents about potential hazards on...

item.title: Modern Amenities and Benefits of New Homes in Myrtle Beach
item.description: Myrtle Beach is a prime destination for those seeking coastal living combined with modern conveniences. New homes in the area offer a blend of contemporary design, energy efficiency, and smart home te...

item.title: Global Oil Crisis Could Accelerate Transition to Renewable Energy
item.description: On 9th March, oil prices surpassed US$100 per barrel for the first time in almost four years due to escalating conflict in West Asia involving the US, Israel, and Iran. Historically, oil shocks trigge...

item.title: Grandmother Loses Custody of Granddaughter Despite Years of Care
item.description: Lilian Evali and her husband Tobias Omondi are grieving after their granddaughter was taken from them following a court ruling that awarded custody to her biological father. Lilian’s late daughter, An...

item.title: Iran-UAE Conflict Claims Lives of Pakistani Labourers, Impacting Gulf Remittances
item.description: In a tragic turn of events, Pakistani workers in the Gulf have been caught in the crossfire of rising tensions between Iran and Gulf countries. Abdul Malick from Jamshoro mourned his nephew, Muzaffar ...

item.title: KTDA board faces leadership changes amid challenges in Kenya’s tea sector
item.description: The Kenya Tea Development Agency (KTDA) is facing yet another round of leadership turmoil with the removal of Chairman Chege Kirundi and the return of former chairman Enos Njeru. Vice-chairman James O...

item.title: Tanzania and Uganda to remove all trade barriers by June to boost trade
item.description: Tanzania and Uganda don reach agreement to remove all trade barriers by June this year, aiming to boost business and economic cooperation between the two countries. The announcement come during offici...

item.title: FASTag Annual Pass Price Hiked to Rs 3,075 from April 1
item.description: Starting April 1, 2026, the FASTag Annual Pass for private vehicles will see a price increase of 2.5%, rising from Rs 3,000 to Rs 3,075. This pass allows car owners to cross up to 200 toll plazas with...

item.title: PTI urges immediate medical transfer of Imran Khan to Shifa International Hospital
item.description: Islamabad: Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) on Friday expressed serious concern over the worsening health of its founder, former Prime Minister Imran Khan. During a press conference, party leaders criti...

item.title: Democrats Eye Kentucky's Open 6th District in 2026 House Race
item.description: Kentucky's 6th Congressional District, a reliably Republican area, is suddenly competitive following two major developments: the retirement of longtime Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell and Rep. ...
